medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Pelvic examination identified a right adnexal mass. Ultrasound demonstrated a 5 cm septated cyst of the right ovary containing multiple mural nodules suggestive of ovarian carcinoma. CT scan revealed a complex cystic right adnexal mass with calcifications and thickened septations, a 7 cm densely calcified mass along the anterior surface of the uterus, and multiple subcentimeter, partially calcified peritoneal masses in the abdomen and pelvis consistent with metastatic disease.

physical_examination.general_examination: Physical examination revealed a 3–4 cm palpable adnexal mass, without evidence of lymphadenopathy, ascites, or abdominal tenderness.
